title: Passive or semi-passive device with polarization conversion capability
abstract: Method and apparatus for passive or semi-passive nodes having polarization conversion capability. The apparatus provides, to a passive node or a semi-passive node, an access control indication that indicates support for at least one of a first mode of operation or a second mode of operation. The apparatus monitors for a backscattered signal from the passive node or the semi-passive node that is supported by the access control indication, where the backscattered signal comprises the first mode of operation. The apparatus communicates with the passive node or the semi-passive node in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus for wireless communication at a network entity, comprising: at least one memory; and at least one processor coupled to the at least one memory and, based at least in part on information stored in the at least one memory, the at least one processor, individually or in any combination, is configured to cause the apparatus to: provide, to a passive node or a semi-passive node, an access control indication that indicates support for at least one of a first mode of operation or a second mode of operation; monitor for a backscattered signal from the passive node or the semipassive node that is supported by the access control indication, wherein the backscattered signal comprises the first mode of operation; and communicate with the passive node or the semi-passive node in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a transceiver coupled to the at least one processor, the transceiver being configured to: provide, to the passive node or the semi-passive node, the access control indication that indicates the support for the at least one of the first mode of operation or the second mode of operation; and communicate with the passive node or the semi-passive node in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein communication with the passive node or the semipassive node does not occur in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal not indicated as supported by the access control indication. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein a first set of communication resources are utilized for the first mode of operation and a second set of communication resources are utilized for the second mode of operation. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein modes of operation comprise at least a first operation with polarization conversion or a second operation without the polarization conversion. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the operation with the polarization conversion provides separation between a query signal provided to the passive node or the semipassive node and the backscattered signal. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the access control indication indicates the support for passive nodes or semi-passive nodes that operate in the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or both the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: obtain, from the passive node or the semi-passive node, a capability indication that indicates the support for at least one of the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the backscattered signal indicates a current mode of operation of the passive node or the semi-passive node. 10. A method of wireless communication at a network entity, comprising: providing, to a passive node or a semi-passive node, an access control indication that indicates support for at least one of a first mode of operation or a second mode of operation; monitoring for a backscattered signal from the passive node or the semi-passive node that is supported by the access control indication, wherein the backscattered signal comprises the first mode of operation; and 

 communicating with the passive node or the semi-passive node in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein communication with the passive node or the semipassive node does not occur in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal not indicated as supported by the access control indication. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein a first set of communication resources are utilized for the first mode of operation and a second set of communication resources are utilized for the second mode of operation. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein modes of operation comprise at least a first operation with polarization conversion or a second operation without the polarization conversion, wherein the operation with the polarization conversion provides separation between a query signal provided to the passive node or the semi-passive node and the backscattered signal. 14. The method of claim 10, wherein the access control indication indicates the support for passive nodes or semi-passive nodes that operate in the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or both the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 15. The method of claim 10, further comprising: obtaining, from the passive node or the semi-passive node, a capability indication that indicates the support for at least one of the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 16. An apparatus for wireless communication at a passive or semi-passive node, comprising: at least one memory; and 

 at least one processor coupled to the at least one memory and, based at least in part on information stored in the at least one memory, the at least one processor, individually or in any combination, is configured to cause the apparatus to: receive, from a network entity, an access control indication that indicates support for at least one of a first mode of operation or a second mode of operation; transmit, to the network entity, a backscattered signal comprising the first mode of operation; and communicate with the network entity in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, further comprising a transceiver coupled to the at least one processor, the transceiver being configured to: receive, from the network entity, the access control indication that indicates the support for the at least one of the first mode of operation or the second mode of operation; transmit, to the network entity, the backscattered signal comprising the first mode of operation; and communicate with the network entity in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 18. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the backscattered signal indicates a mode of operation of the backscattered signal. 19. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: switch between the first mode of operation or the second mode of operation based on the access control indication. 20. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein communication with the network entity does not occur in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal not indicated as supported by the access control indication. 21. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein modes of operation comprise at least a first operation with polarization conversion or a second operation without the polarization conversion. 22. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the operation with the polarization conversion provides separation between a query signal provided to the passive or semi-passive node and the backscattered signal. 23. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the access control indication indicates the support for passive nodes or semi-passive nodes that operate in the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or both the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 24. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: transmit, to the network entity, a capability indication that indicates the support for at least one of the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation. 25. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the backscattered signal indicates a current mode of operation of the passive or semi-passive node. 26. A method of wireless communication at a passive or semi-passive node, comprising: receiving, from a network entity, an access control indication that indicates support for at least one of a first mode of operation or a second mode of operation; transmitting, to the network entity, a backscattered signal comprising the first mode of operation; and communicating with the network entity in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal actively supported by the access control indication. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the backscattered signal indicates a mode of operation of the backscattered signal. 28. The method of claim 26, further comprising: 

 switching between the first mode of operation or the second mode of operation based on the access control indication. 29. The method of claim 26, wherein communication with the network entity does not occur in response to the first mode of operation of the backscattered signal not indicated as supported by the access control indication. 30. The method of claim 26, further comprising: transmitting, to the network entity, a capability indication that indicates the support for at least one of the first mode of operation, the second mode of operation, or the first mode of operation and the second mode of operation.
